data.title: Roivant Sciences GAAP EPS of -$0.29 misses by $0.07, revenue of $7.57M
data.description:
• Roivant Sciences press release (NASDAQ:ROIV): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.29 misses by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $7.57M (-16.1% Y/Y).

data.title: Earnings snapshot: Hormel Foods beats bottom-line estimates; updates FY25 outlook
Kohl's reports mixed Q1 results; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Kohl's press release (NYSE:KSS): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.13 beats by $0.09.
  • Revenue of $3B (-11.2% Y/Y) misses by $60M; comparable sales decreased 3.9.
  • Inventory was $3.1 billion, an increase of 2% year-over-year.
  • Operating cash flow was a use of $92 million.
  • 2025 Financial and Capital Allocation Outlook: For the full year 2025, the Company continues to expect the following, excluding the impact of potential items not representative of our core operating performance:
    • Net sales: A decrease of (5%) to a decrease of (7%)  vs. estimated growth of -4.30% Y/Y.
    • Comparable sales: A decrease of (4%) to a decrease of (6%)
    • Operating margin: In the range of 2.2% to 2.6%
    • Diluted EPS: In the range of $0.10 to $0.60 vs. $0.49 consensus.
    • Capital Expenditures: In the range of $400 million to $425 million.
    • Dividend: On May 14, 2025, Kohl’s Board of Directors declared a quarterly cash dividend on the Company’s common stock of $0.125 per share. The dividend is payable June 25, 2025 to shareholders of record at the close of business on June 11, 2025.

data.title: SpartanNash Non-GAAP EPS of $0.35 misses by $0.07, revenue of $2.91B beats by $50M
data.description:
• SpartanNash press release (NASDAQ:SPTN): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.35 misses by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $2.91B (+3.6% Y/Y) beats by $50M.
  • Net sales increased 3.7% to $2.91 billion, driven by an increase in volume in the Retail segment, partially offset by lower volume in the Wholesale segment.
  • FY25 sales consensus of $9.91B, EPS consensus of $1.76

data.title: Movado Non-GAAP EPS of $0.08, revenue of $131.8M misses by $10.31M
data.description:
• Movado press release (NYSE:MOV): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.08.
  • Revenue of $131.8M (-1.9% Y/Y) misses by $10.31M.
  • Ended the quarter with cash of $203.1 million and no debt.

  • During the first quarter of fiscal 2026, the Company did not repurchase shares under its December 5, 2024 share repurchase program. As of April 30, 2025, the Company had $50.0 million remaining available under the share repurchase program.

  • Fiscal 2026 Outlook

    Given the current economic uncertainty and the unpredictable impact of tariff developments on the Company’s business, the Company has elected not to provide a fiscal 2026 outlook at this time. However, the Company is planning to take actions to partially mitigate the impact of the recent tariff changes, including select price increases at the wholesale and retail levels.

data.title: Stock index futures gain as court strikes down Trump's April tariffs; Nvidia results bring cheer
data.description:
Stock index futures pared some gains on Thursday as the rally driven by a court striking down President Trump's April tariffs seemed to fade, but Nvidia's (NVDA) results and guidance continued to lift the mood.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +1.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +1.9%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.6%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 4 basis points to 4.53%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 4 basis points to 4.04%.

A federal trade court has struck down the “reciprocal” tariffs that Trump imposed on what he called “Liberation Day,” putting an immediate stop to the collection of a sweeping set of levies that the president imposed.

“We should say at the outset that this (the court ruling) is hardly the end of this story, as the administration are appealing this decision. But already, markets have seen a substantial rally in response,” Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

AI darling Nvidia (NVDA) rose 5.3% in premarket trade after the firm reported fiscal first-quarter results and guidance that topped expectations, despite the impact of added export control curbs to China.

“The tariff developments have overshadowed the previous day’s news, but sentiment also got a boost from Nvidia’s results after the U.S. close yesterday, as the company delivered upbeat sales guidance,” Allen added.

For now, the court ruling lifts a significant tax burden from US consumers and companies. However, the main outcome is uncertainty, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Looking towards the economic calendar now, there are a few macroeconomic data along with several Federal Reserve speakers lined up on Thursday.

The initial jobless claims report is expected to land before the opening bell and is anticipated to rise to 229K.

The Q1 GDP will also be released at the same time and is expected to show a 0.3% annualized contraction. 

“We get revised first-quarter US GDP data today—an imprecise number and distorted by front-loaded purchases by companies and consumers,” Donovan said.

The April pending home sales report will land during market hours and is expected to come in at -0.9% M/M.

Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee and San Francisco Fed President Mary Daly, along with a few other Fed officials, will be speaking later in the day at different times and events. 

Wall Street ended lower Wednesday, with a noticeable downward move in the final hour of trading triggered by a report that the U.S. government had ordered chip designers to stop selling to China.

data.title: UniCredit pays over €600 million for new Alpha Bank stake - report
data.description:
UniCredit SpA (OTCPK:UNCFF) reportedly paid slightly more than €600 million ($677 million) to double its stake in Greece’s Alpha Services and Holdings SA.

The Italian lender announced Wednesday that it bought a  stake of around 9.7% in the Greek bank, bringing its total holding to nearly 20%.

The transaction cost around €615 million, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

UniCredit SpA (OTCPK:UNCFF) (OTCPK:UNCRY) has been actively exploring acquisition opportunities in recent months under CEO Andrea Orcel, as he aims to position the bank at the forefront of the consolidation trend reshaping Europe’s banking and insurance landscape.

However, UniCredit’s efforts haven’t always been well-received. Its approach to Germany’s Commerzbank (OTCPK:CRZBF) has faced political resistance, while its bid to acquire domestic rival Banco BPM (OTCPK:BNCZF) has also encountered challenges.

data.title: Build-A-Bear Workshop GAAP EPS of $1.17 beats by $0.31, revenue of $128.4M beats by $9.5M
data.description:
• Build-A-Bear Workshop press release (NYSE:BBW): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.17 beats by $0.31.
  • Revenue of $128.4M (+11.9% Y/Y) beats by $9.5M.
  • Net retail sales were $119.6 million and increased 10.9%
  • Consolidated e-commerce demand (online orders fulfilled from either the Company’s warehouses or its stores) increased 0.5%
  • Commercial and international franchise revenues were a combined $8.8 million and increased 28.3%

  • The Company reiterates its 2025 revenue guidance and updates its pre-tax income guidance, inclusive of current tariff rates. Specifically, the Company:

    • Continues to expect total revenues to grow on a mid-single-digit percentage basis
    • Now expects pre-tax income in the range of $61 million to $67 million

data.title: Kraken Robotics reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Kraken Robotics press release (OTCQB:KRKNF): Q1 Consolidated revenue decreased 23% to C$16.1 million, compared to C$20.9 in the prior year. The decrease was driven by a year-over-year decline in Product revenue.
  • EPS of C$0.01
